software_it.label: Software & IT
software_it.explainer: When AI coding assistants and agent frameworks surge, it signals that the tools developers use daily are becoming more autonomous. Research from Stanford and ADP shows that entry-level software developer roles contracted between late 2022 and mid-2025, while demand for senior developers remained stable or grew. These tools amplify experienced developers while reducing demand for junior ones.

legal.label: Legal & Compliance
legal.explainer: These tools convert stacks of documents into structured data that AI can analyze. When adoption accelerates, it means companies are building systems to automate document review, contract analysis, regulatory compliance checks, and due diligence — tasks currently done by paralegals, junior associates, and compliance staff. Harvey AI (a legal AI startup) has raised $300M+, signaling major venture capital conviction that these workflows will be automated.

finance.label: Finance & Insurance
finance.explainer: Financial services runs on documents — loan applications, insurance claims, regulatory filings, tax returns. When AI document processing tools accelerate, it signals automation of the workflows that employ millions of back-office workers in banking, insurance, and accounting. J.P. Morgan research found a mildly negative correlation between AI usage and employment trends in financial services subsectors.

healthcare.label: Healthcare
healthcare.explainer: Healthcare AI tools are growing slowly compared to other industries. This makes sense — healthcare has high regulatory barriers (HIPAA, FDA), requires clinical validation, and stakes are literally life and death. The slow signal here suggests healthcare workers face less near-term automation pressure from open-source AI tools. However, proprietary tools (like ambient clinical documentation from Abridge or Nuance/DAX) are growing fast — they just don't show up in this open-source data.

creative.label: Creative & Media
creative.explainer: AI image generation had explosive growth in 2022-2023 but has recently stabilized (diffusers actually declined recently). Meanwhile, speech-to-text tools continue growing steadily. The creative industry signal is nuanced: the initial wave of AI image generation disrupted stock photography and simple illustration, but more complex creative work has been harder to automate. The stabilization in image generation tools may reflect market maturation rather than declining impact.

office.label: Office & Administrative
office.explainer: These are the broadest signals — general-purpose AI agents and automation tools that can be applied to any office workflow. When browser automation (browser-use) or multi-agent frameworks (crewai) surge, it signals that developers are building tools to replace repetitive tasks across all administrative functions. This is the category most aligned with the "AI is coming for white-collar work" narrative, and the one where employment data is most worth watching.

customer_service.label: Customer Service & Support
customer_service.explainer: When conversational AI and voice agent frameworks surge, it signals that companies are building systems to automate customer interactions — chatbots replacing live chat, voice agents replacing call center workers, and AI copilots handling tier-1 support tickets. Call center employment (BLS NAICS 56142) has been declining steadily, and the rapid growth of open-source chatbot frameworks confirms the trend: enterprises are deploying AI agents that handle customer queries 24/7 at a fraction of the cost of human agents.

education.label: Education & Training
education.explainer: AI is entering education through multiple vectors: automated grading tools, AI-powered tutoring systems, and interactive learning platforms built on LLMs. When tools like nbgrader (auto-grading) and AI SDKs surge in edtech contexts, it signals that educational institutions and edtech companies are building systems that can personalize instruction, assess student work, and generate curriculum content — tasks currently performed by teachers, tutors, and instructional designers.

manufacturing.label: Manufacturing & Logistics
manufacturing.explainer: Computer vision and robotics frameworks are the key AI signals for manufacturing and logistics. When tools like YOLO (ultralytics) and OpenCV surge, it means companies are deploying automated visual inspection, defect detection, warehouse robotics, and logistics tracking systems. Unlike white-collar AI tools, these directly automate physical-world tasks — quality control on production lines, autonomous warehouse navigation, and robotic manipulation.

sales_marketing.label: Sales & Marketing
sales_marketing.explainer: AI is rapidly transforming sales and marketing through automated lead scoring, personalized email generation, AI-powered CRM workflows, and data-driven campaign optimization. When LLM orchestration tools and CRM SDKs surge together, it signals that sales teams are building AI pipelines that can qualify leads, draft outreach, analyze customer intent, and optimize marketing spend — replacing work done by SDRs, copywriters, and marketing analysts.

packages.name: openai
packages.label: The core Python SDK for calling OpenAI models (GPT-4, o1, DALL-E). Used as the foundation for most AI applications — when this grows, overall AI adoption is growing.

packages.name: anthropic
packages.label: Python SDK for Anthropic's Claude models. Growing rapidly as Claude gains market share in enterprise AI, especially for long-document analysis and coding tasks.

packages.name: langchain
packages.label: The most popular framework for building AI-powered applications. Provides the plumbing that connects LLMs to databases, APIs, and documents — a general indicator of AI app development activity.

packages.name: langchain-core
packages.label: The foundational building blocks of LangChain — chains, prompts, output parsers. Installed as a dependency by virtually every LangChain-based project.

packages.name: transformers
packages.label: Hugging Face's library for running open-source AI models locally. The default choice for teams that need to self-host models rather than use cloud APIs — common in regulated industries.

packages.name: openai-npm
packages.label: JavaScript SDK for OpenAI. Used to add GPT-4 and other OpenAI models to web applications, chatbots, and Node.js backends.

packages.name: @anthropic-ai/sdk
packages.label: JavaScript SDK for Anthropic's Claude. Used by web developers building Claude-powered features into websites and web applications.

packages.name: aider-chat
packages.label: An AI pair programmer that runs in your terminal and edits code directly in your repo. Developers use it to write features, fix bugs, and refactor code through natural language — replacing some of the work junior developers do.

packages.name: browser-use
packages.label: Lets AI agents control a web browser — clicking buttons, filling forms, navigating pages. Used to automate repetitive web-based tasks that currently require a human at a keyboard.

packages.name: crewai
packages.label: A framework for orchestrating multiple AI agents that collaborate on complex tasks. Each agent has a role (researcher, writer, reviewer) and they pass work between each other — mimicking how human teams operate.

packages.name: autogen
packages.label: Microsoft's framework for building multi-agent AI systems where agents can have conversations, write code, and coordinate to solve complex problems without human intervention.

packages.name: pyautogen
packages.label: Alternate distribution of Microsoft AutoGen. Same multi-agent capabilities — tracked separately because both package names see significant download volume.

packages.name: smolagents
packages.label: Hugging Face's lightweight agent framework. Designed for quick automation tasks where a full multi-agent system is overkill — one AI agent that can call tools and take actions.

packages.name: ai
packages.label: Vercel's AI SDK — the most popular way to add streaming AI chat, text generation, and tool use to React and Next.js web apps. Its growth tracks how fast AI is being embedded into production web products.

packages.name: @ai-sdk/openai
packages.label: Connects Vercel's AI SDK to OpenAI models. When developers build AI features into web apps using the AI SDK, this is one of the most common provider packages they install.

packages.name: @ai-sdk/anthropic
packages.label: Connects Vercel's AI SDK to Anthropic Claude models. Growth here reflects Claude adoption specifically within production web applications.

packages.name: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
packages.label: The official SDK for Model Context Protocol — an emerging open standard that lets AI models connect to external tools, databases, and APIs. Its explosive growth signals that AI tool integration is being standardized.

packages.name: playwright-npm
packages.label: Microsoft's browser automation framework — the backbone of AI agent systems that need to interact with web pages. When AI agents need to click, type, and navigate websites, Playwright is the most common tool under the hood.

packages.name: puppeteer-npm
packages.label: Google's browser automation library for Chrome/Chromium. A predecessor to Playwright that remains widely used for web scraping, testing, and building AI-powered browser agents.

packages.name: docling
packages.label: IBM's document conversion library that turns PDFs, Word docs, and scanned images into structured data an AI can analyze. Heavily used in legal tech for processing contracts, court filings, and regulatory documents.

packages.name: marker-pdf
packages.label: Converts PDFs into clean Markdown text while preserving structure like headings, tables, and lists. Critical for legal and compliance workflows where document formatting carries meaning.

packages.name: unstructured
packages.label: An ETL pipeline for documents — ingests PDFs, emails, HTML, images, and Office files, then outputs clean structured data for AI processing. Used across industries wherever large document backlogs need to be made AI-readable.

packages.name: pypdf
packages.label: The standard Python library for reading and manipulating PDF files. A foundational dependency in any workflow that processes contracts, financial statements, or regulatory filings at scale.

packages.name: llamaindex
packages.label: JavaScript SDK for LlamaIndex — builds AI systems that can search, summarize, and answer questions across large document collections. Used in legal tech for contract review and research automation.

packages.name: @langchain/core
packages.label: The JavaScript core of LangChain — provides chains, agents, and retrieval tools for building AI document processing pipelines in web applications. A cross-industry building block for AI-powered workflows.

packages.name: @langchain/openai
packages.label: Connects LangChain's JavaScript framework to OpenAI models. Used in production web apps that need GPT-4 for document analysis, data extraction, and automated decision-making workflows.

packages.name: pandasai
packages.label: Lets users query spreadsheets and databases using plain English instead of writing code. An analyst can ask 'show me Q3 revenue by region' and get the answer — automating work that previously required SQL or Excel expertise.

packages.name: dataprep
packages.label: Automates the tedious work of cleaning, validating, and preparing datasets for analysis. Handles missing values, type conversions, and formatting — tasks that data analysts and junior quants spend hours on manually.

packages.name: llama-index
packages.label: Python framework for building 'retrieval-augmented generation' (RAG) systems — AI that answers questions by searching through your own documents first. Used in finance for research, compliance, and due diligence automation.

packages.name: chromadb
packages.label: An open-source vector database that stores document embeddings for AI-powered search. When financial firms build AI systems to search across thousands of research reports or filings, ChromaDB is a common backend choice.

packages.name: medspacy
packages.label: A clinical NLP toolkit built on spaCy that extracts structured data from unstructured medical records — identifying diagnoses, medications, and procedures from doctor's notes and discharge summaries.

packages.name: scispacy
packages.label: NLP models trained on biomedical text — can identify drug names, gene references, and disease mentions in research papers and clinical documents. Used in pharmaceutical research and clinical data extraction.

packages.name: diffusers
packages.label: Hugging Face's library for running Stable Diffusion, FLUX, and other AI image generation models. The main open-source tool that lets developers generate, edit, and transform images with AI — widely used in design and media production.

packages.name: replicate
packages.label: A cloud platform for running AI models via API — especially popular for image generation, video creation, and audio processing. Developers use it when they want AI creative tools without managing GPU infrastructure.

packages.name: stability-sdk
packages.label: Stability AI's official SDK for their image generation models (Stable Diffusion). Used by developers building AI-powered design tools, marketing asset generators, and creative automation pipelines.

packages.name: faster-whisper
packages.label: An optimized implementation of OpenAI's Whisper speech-to-text model that runs 4x faster. Used to transcribe podcasts, meetings, video content, and interviews — automating work done by human transcriptionists.

packages.name: openai-whisper
packages.label: OpenAI's original Whisper model for speech recognition and transcription. Supports 99 languages and is used for subtitling, podcast transcription, voice-to-text features, and accessibility tools.

packages.name: replicate-npm
packages.label: JavaScript client for Replicate's AI model platform. Used by web developers to add AI image generation, video processing, and audio tools directly into websites and web applications.

packages.name: continuedev
packages.label: Open-source AI code assistant (like Copilot but extensible) — tracks developer adoption of AI pair programming beyond GitHub Copilot. Supports multiple LLM backends and IDE integrations.

packages.name: fal-client
packages.label: Client for fal.ai's inference platform — used for fast image and video generation, signaling creative production automation and the shift toward API-driven media creation workflows.

packages.name: audiocraft
packages.label: Meta's audio and music generation library — signals AI displacement in music production, sound design, and audio content creation. Includes MusicGen and AudioGen models.

packages.name: yt-dlp
packages.label: Media download tool heavily used in AI training data pipelines and automated content processing workflows. Its growth reflects the expanding infrastructure for AI-powered media analysis and content creation.

packages.name: monai
packages.label: PyTorch framework for medical imaging AI — used in radiology, pathology, and diagnostic imaging automation. Its adoption signals how quickly AI is entering clinical diagnostic workflows.

packages.name: biopython
packages.label: Bioinformatics toolkit — signals AI-assisted genomics, drug discovery, and clinical research automation. A foundational library for computational biology workflows increasingly augmented by ML.

packages.name: ta-lib
packages.label: Technical analysis library for financial markets — when combined with ML frameworks, signals AI-driven trading strategy automation and algorithmic decision-making in finance.

packages.name: yfinance
packages.label: Yahoo Finance data API — growth reflects automated financial data ingestion for AI-powered analysis, trading bots, and portfolio management systems.

packages.name: pyod
packages.label: Python Outlier Detection library with 40+ algorithms — signals AI automation of fraud detection, financial compliance monitoring, and risk assessment workflows that previously required manual review.

packages.name: botpress
packages.label: Open-source chatbot and conversational AI platform used to build customer-facing virtual agents that replace live chat and phone support. Its growth tracks enterprise adoption of AI-powered customer service.

packages.name: rasa
packages.label: Open-source conversational AI framework for building contextual AI assistants — used heavily in enterprise customer service automation for intent classification, dialogue management, and entity extraction.

packages.name: vocode
packages.label: Open-source library for building voice-based AI agents (phone bots, IVR replacements) — signals automation of call center work and the shift from human to AI voice interactions.

packages.name: chainlit
packages.label: Framework for building conversational AI interfaces — used to create customer-facing chat support tools powered by LLMs, enabling rapid deployment of AI support agents.

packages.name: gradio
packages.label: Builds interactive web UIs for AI models — increasingly used for customer-facing AI tools, internal support dashboards, and educational AI interfaces like student-facing tutors and interactive demos.

packages.name: streamlit
packages.label: Turns Python scripts into interactive web apps — widely used to build AI-powered learning tools, tutoring interfaces, and training dashboards in educational settings.

packages.name: nbgrader
packages.label: Jupyter-based auto-grading tool — signals AI-assisted assessment automation in education. Used by universities and online course platforms to automate homework and exam grading.

packages.name: sentence-transformers
packages.label: Produces text embeddings used in educational search, plagiarism detection, and adaptive learning systems. Growth signals AI-powered content understanding in edtech applications.

packages.name: ultralytics
packages.label: YOLO object detection framework — the dominant tool for visual inspection, defect detection, and warehouse automation in manufacturing. Its growth directly tracks computer vision deployment in industrial settings.

packages.name: opencv-python
packages.label: The foundational computer vision library — used in quality control, robotics guidance, and logistics tracking systems. A baseline indicator of computer vision adoption across manufacturing and logistics.

packages.name: supervision
packages.label: Computer vision utilities for detection, tracking, and annotation — used in manufacturing line monitoring and warehouse automation. Simplifies building production-ready CV pipelines.

packages.name: open3d
packages.label: 3D data processing library used in robotic manipulation, warehouse mapping, and manufacturing digital twins. Growth signals increasing adoption of 3D perception in industrial automation.

packages.name: robotframework
packages.label: Generic test and RPA automation framework increasingly used for manufacturing process automation and logistics workflow orchestration. Its growth in industrial contexts signals process automation beyond software.

packages.name: instructor
packages.label: Structured output extraction from LLMs — used in sales and marketing for parsing leads, extracting intent from customer messages, and generating structured campaign data at scale.

packages.name: outlines
packages.label: Structured text generation from LLMs — used for generating marketing copy, product descriptions, and sales collateral at scale with guaranteed output formats.

packages.name: posthog-js
packages.label: PostHog product analytics SDK with AI features — signals AI-driven marketing optimization, user segmentation, and campaign targeting. Tracks how companies use behavioral data to automate marketing decisions.

packages.name: @hubspot/api-client
packages.label: HubSpot CRM SDK — growth signals AI-powered CRM automation for sales pipeline management and marketing workflow orchestration. Tracks programmatic integration of AI into sales processes.
